meta.maintenanceInstruction: KEEP THE PLAN UP TO DATE AS YOU EVOLVE THE DESIGN. DEVIATIONS TO THE PLAN MUST BE LOGGED WITHIN THE PLAN. THE PLAN MUST BE REASSESSED HOLISTICALLY AFTER ANY CHANGE OF ANY SIZE.
meta.completionPolicy: YOU ARE NOT DONE UNTIL YOU HAVE COMPLETED 100% OF THE BLUEPRINT. DO NOT STOP. YOU MUST REFINE AT REGULAR INTERVALS TO KEEP THE BLUEPRINT FULL. NEVER SPECIFY ANY ESTIMATE OF EFFORT OR COST — ESPECIALLY NOT TIME ESTIMATES. NEVER SPECIFY A PRIORITY OF ANY KIND.

globalAlerts.warning: DESIGN PHASE: This blueprint tracks UI/UX design work for the slop-funnel TUI in OpenPencil MCP. All design changes are made in the Figma/OpenPencil document via MCP tools, not in code files.

PRODUCT CONTEXT: slop-funnel performs EXHAUSTIVE extraction of value from 1-N divergent git branches. Every hunk from every branch diff must be explicitly considered (merged, discarded, or marked with notes). Like an external sort — changes grouped by agentic AI, staged in a workflow loop with backpressure. Must support claude and other agents via ACP (https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/agent-communication-protocol).

TERMINOLOGY (CONFIRMED): 'Chunk' = a group of 1-N hunks from any/all source branches, reviewable as one unit by the human-in-the-loop. A chunk's 'Patch' is the diff/patch that is the mergable payload. The agent may REIMPLEMENT the patch — chunks are NOT just 'apply hunks verbatim'. Even chunks where ALL hunks are discarded must still flow through human-in-the-loop review. A chunk is never silently skipped. This is the core guarantee of 'exhaustive'.
INTEGRATION (CONFIRMED): slop-funnel will be a new osm subcommand (e.g. `osm slop-funnel`), living in the one-shot-man repo, using osm's Go framework, scripting runtime, and session management.

SESSION STATE (CONFIRMED): All slop-funnel state is tracked in osm sessions (`osm session id` etc). The TUI reads/writes session state. This includes: branch selections, chunk status, commit history, agent interactions, and user decisions.

LAUNCH FLOW (CONFIRMED): Branches are selected entirely within the TUI — not via CLI args. May also support a go-prompt terminal mode bootstrapped like `osm super-document`, with seamless transition between 'terminal-style TUI' and 'advanced interactive user interface TUI'. The entire experience is interactive.

CHUNK LIFECYCLE (CONFIRMED): Chunks are staged up by a workflow-style orchestration with backpressure. Chunks can be rejected with a reason. The orchestrator stages an appropriate number of chunks while the user works. One chunk = one commit. Agent integration details TBD (to be refined over time).

AGENT INTERACTION (CONFIRMED): There MUST be feedback and interaction. Higher-level than osm pr-split. May need to surface agent's train of thought for debugging/guidance. Details TBD — dependent on agentic tool capabilities, requiring exploration and research.

CHUNK REVIEW FLOW (CONFIRMED): Default behavior stages the diff on top of the current checked-out branch (trunk) using `git add` via go-git. User views diff in their OWN tooling (like interactive rebasing). The TUI does NOT embed a diff viewer — it orchestrates the staging, the user reviews externally.

CHUNK REJECTION MODEL (CONFIRMED — COMPLEX): Rejection is NOT simple accept/reject. It means: (1) 'AI did a bad job — restructure this chunk' with specific reasons. (2) If the chunk BOUNDARY is sensible, it should never need rejection. (3) May need to expose RAW hunks to the user or other AI tools. (4) Can reject SPECIFIC HUNKS within a chunk — 'this hunk should be with X'. (5) Multiple rejection reasons for different subsets/hunks within a single chunk. (6) Some hunks may not be rejected while others are. (7) Rejected hunks are put BACK INTO THE POOL to be re-grouped and re-prepared for human-in-the-loop processing. This is a core UX challenge.

TRUNK/COMMIT MODEL (CONFIRMED): Commits go directly to the trunk branch. Typical flow: trunk checked out, potentially in a worktree. User's checked-out branch state should be as STABLE and SAFE as possible.

BRANCH PICKING (CONFIRMED): For Takumi to explore and propose. Ultimately just a way to derive a set of hunks. Recording the LINEAGE/SOURCE of hunks with contextual structure is CRITICAL — may require special handling for future features. Takumi must prove competence through discovery.

COMPLETION MODEL (CONFIRMED): Exhaustive + resumable. ALL hunks from ALL source branches must be resolved (committed or explicitly discarded with notes) before session is 'done'. User can PAUSE and resume later via session. 100% exhaustive completion is the guarantee.

CONFLICT MODEL (CONFIRMED): Chunks that conflict with trunk are NOT ready for human-in-the-loop. Both the UI and agent must be AWARE of conflicts. Agent addresses conflicts and may reject chunks at its own discretion (e.g. based on user-implemented pivots). Conflicts are an internal orchestration concern, not a user-facing blocking issue.

UNDO/REVERT (CONFIRMED — LATER FEATURE): User can revert a committed chunk. Revert is orchestrated by slop-funnel and includes returning hunks from that chunk BACK TO THE POOL. This is a feature for later, not MVP.

SESSION PERSISTENCE (CONFIRMED — TWO TIERS): (1) 'Exit then reopen' = ephemeral but robust, provided by core osm session store. (2) 'Resume' = later feature, handles source branch changes, robust enough to transport between machines/OS/AI tools. These are distinct features.
APPROVE FLOW (CONFIRMED): Simple — 'yes, that chunk has been handled'. Chunk doesn't surface again in pool or chunk list. User stages up the commit externally, then confirms in TUI.
CHUNK DEPENDENCIES (CONFIRMED): Chunks may have dependencies. Agent indicates dependencies. Dependencies are used for queuing logic (queuing for processing).
AGENT SECTION (CONFIRMED): Collapsible section. Default collapsed, just shows a status badge. Expand for detail on what agent is doing.
PRODUCT NAME (CONFIRMED): 'slop-funnel' is the FINAL name. Descriptive — 'literally how it works, and also what it alleviates — dumbasses pouring AI down our proverbial throats.'

CHUNK LIST DESIGN (CONFIRMED): Split list + detail view. Top section = scrollable chunk queue. Bottom section = selected chunk detail (branches, files, deps, agent notes, hunk breakdown). Split on click or select. Both mouse AND keyboard navigation. Viewport and lists have scrollbars. Existing TUI examples in the project (pr-split, super-document) should inform the design.
DIRECTIVE: Any information Hana-san gives via answers MUST be accumulated in this blueprint immediately. Do not lose context.
HANA-SAN IS THE PRODUCT MANAGER. Takumi is the designer/engineer. Product direction questions MUST be asked of Hana-san before proceeding. Do not assume product direction — ask.
SCREEN1 IS IN OPENPENCIL MCP: Page 'Page 1', node ID '0:6' (720×375). It is the main dashboard view. There is also a 'Components' page (currently empty).

mandatoryDirectives.contextManagement: This is a collaborative design session with Hana-san. Ask questions using AskUserQuestion tool. Iterate on Screen1 in the MCP. Update WIP.md with findings and decisions. Do not proceed past ambiguities — surface them as questions.
mandatoryDirectives.workingDirectory: docs/projects/slop-funnel/ — blueprint.json and WIP.md live HERE, not in the project root.
mandatoryDirectives.mcpLocation: OpenPencil MCP. Page 1 has Screen1 (node 0:6). Components page is empty and reserved for the component library.

statusSection.currentState: Screen1 fully rebuilt: zero contrast errors, zero overflow issues, all nodes semantically named, 8px grid compliance. Design tokens complete: 37 COLOR variables in `slop-funnel` collection, all 115 colored nodes bound. Chunk list + detail split view designed. Product direction clarified through 5 rounds of Q&A. Next: create component library on Components page (Task 8).
statusSection.goalState: Screen1 is fully refined with proper contrast, layout, spacing, and naming. Product direction is clarified. All screens for the slop-funnel TUI are designed with a component library and defined interaction patterns. The design is ready for implementation handoff.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix Screen1 color contrast errors: 14 dark-on-dark text instances across SessionBar, ActionHint, ChunkStatus rows, ProgressSection, and StatusBar
sequentialTasks.description: The describe tool identified 14 instances where text is too dark against its background: SessionLabel (#888 on #1A1A2E), SessionSep (#444 on #1A1A2E), SessionTime (#888 on #1A1A2E), RepoLabel (#888 on #1A1A2E), MenuHelp (#888 on #0D0D0D), HintText (#888 on #000), HintSep (#444 on #000), ActionCommitText (#888 on #333), ActionPauseText (#888 on #333), ActionQuitText (#888 on #333), ProgressLabel (#888 on #000), ProgressFiles (#666 on #000), StatusHint (#666 on #1E1E1E), StatusChunks (#888 on #1E1E1E), StatusSep (#444 on #1E1E1E), StatusWorktree (#888 on #1E1E1E). Fix each by raising text color to at least #AAAAAA for secondary text and #CCCCCC for primary readable text. Node IDs: 0:23, 0:25, 0:29, 0:27, 0:19, 0:60, 0:61, 0:95, 0:97, 0:99, 0:84, 0:88, 0:101, 0:102, 0:103, 0:104.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Re-running describe on Screen1 (node 0:6) produces zero color contrast 'error' severity issues. All text is legible against its background — secondary/muted text at minimum #999999, primary text at minimum #BBBBBB, on their respective dark backgrounds.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix Screen1 layout overflow: Frame children (100px tall) overflow StatusRows parent rows (24px tall) in ChunkStatus section
sequentialTasks.description: COMPLETED. Resized overflow frames (0:66, 0:72, 0:78) from 100px height to 8px height. Renamed to ReviewProgress, StagedProgress, CommittedProgress. Also fixed BranchSummary gap from 6 to 8px, Content gap standardized to 8px, ChunkHeader/ProgressHeader/ProgressDetail set to FILL horizontal, BottomSpacer deleted, Menu padding adjusted, HeaderTitle changed to 'slop-funnel'.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Re-running describe on node 0:62 produces zero 'overflow' severity issues. All children fit within their parent bounds. The visual spacing in ChunkStatus StatusRows is clean and intentional.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix Screen1 structural issues: inconsistent gaps, generic Frame names, HUG+justify warnings, BottomSpacer orphan
sequentialTasks.description: COMPLETED. All generic 'Frame' nodes renamed to semantic names (SessionInfo, RepoInfo, BranchRow, ReviewProgress, StagedProgress, CommittedProgress, ProgressHeader, ProgressDetail). Content and BranchSummary gaps standardized to 8px. ChunkHeader, ProgressHeader, ProgressDetail set to FILL horizontal. BottomSpacer deleted. HeaderTitle changed to 'slop-funnel'.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Re-running describe on Screen1 (0:6) produces zero structural 'warning' or 'info' severity issues related to naming, gaps, or layout. All frames have semantic names. All gaps are on 8px grid. HUG+justify frames are either given w='fill' or justify is removed. BottomSpacer is deleted.

sequentialTasks.task: Clarify product direction: ask Hana-san key questions about slop-funnel's workflow, screens, and interaction model
sequentialTasks.description: COMPLETED 5 ROUNDS OF Q&A. Key decisions captured: chunk terminology, launch flow, chunk lifecycle, agent interaction, rejection model, trunk writes, completion model, conflict model, session persistence, approve flow, dependencies, agent section visibility, product name, chunk list design (split list + detail view). See globalAlerts and WIP.md for full details. All product direction questions resolved. Hana-san confirmed: split list + detail, mouse + keyboard nav, scrollbars, design system tokens needed.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All product direction questions are answered and recorded in WIP.md. The answers are specific enough to drive screen design decisions. No ambiguity remains about the core workflow, the number and purpose of screens, or the interaction model.

sequentialTasks.task: Refine Screen1 information architecture based on product direction answers
sequentialTasks.description: COMPLETED. Screen1 fully rebuilt (Content frame 0:182) with correct IA: SessionBar (0:183) with session ID, repo, time; BranchSummary (0:192) with trunk + source branch cards color-coded by branch; ChunkPane (0:217) with split list+detail view; Actions bar (0:289) with Approve/Reject/ExternalDiff/Pause/Quit; StatusBar (0:302). All contrast errors fixed (19 set_fill operations). All 70+ generic names replaced with semantic names. All spacing brought to 8px grid. Layout collapse warnings resolved with FILL sizing. Zero error-severity issues in describe validation.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Screen1's sections accurately reflect the confirmed slop-funnel workflow. Every section maps to a confirmed product requirement. The chunk list is a real interactive list, not just status counts. Agent section is present and collapsible. Header says 'slop-funnel'. Describe tool shows no structural or content issues.

sequentialTasks.task: Design and propose the chunk list UI component for Hana-san review
sequentialTasks.description: IN PROGRESS. Chunk list + detail split view designed in Screen1's ChunkPane (0:217). ChunkQueue (0:223) shows 5 chunk rows with: status dot (●/◌/✗), chunk ID, title, status badge (READY/BLOCKED/REJECTED), dependency arrows, branch source color indicators, scroll indicator. ChunkDetail (0:267) shows selected chunk's: header with title+badge, meta with source/dep info, file list, hunk breakdown with line ranges and check marks, agent notes section. 5 states designed: ready (green, #28C840), blocked (orange, #FFBD2E), rejected (red, #FF8888), with selected row highlight (#002200 bg). Awaiting Hana-san review of the design to fulfill acceptance criteria.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A designed chunk list component exists in Screen1 (or as a separate component on the Components page). Hana-san has reviewed it and provided feedback. The design shows how individual chunks are presented, how status is indicated, and how dependencies are visualized.

sequentialTasks.task: Design Screen2+: identify and create additional TUI screens based on product direction
sequentialTasks.description: Based on product direction answers, identify all screens the TUI needs beyond the main dashboard. Likely candidates: chunk detail/review screen, diff viewer, branch selection/config screen, agent activity monitor, conflict resolution screen. Create each screen as a new top-level frame on Page 1 or a new page. Each screen must follow the same design system (colors, fonts, spacing, component patterns) as the refined Screen1. Screens will be designed iteratively with Hana-san feedback.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All required screens exist in the OpenPencil document. Each screen has a clear purpose documented in WIP.md. Navigation between screens is defined. All screens use consistent design tokens and component patterns. Describe tool shows no errors on any screen.

sequentialTasks.task: Create component library on the Components page in OpenPencil
sequentialTasks.description: The 'Components' page (0:106) is currently empty. Populate it with reusable TUI components: Header component, MenuBar, SessionBar, BranchCard (trunk + source variants), ChunkRow (review/staged/committed states), ProgressBar (with fill states), ActionButton (primary/secondary/disabled variants), StatusBar, Divider, Badge/Pill, Icon indicators. Each component should be a proper OpenPencil COMPONENT node with defined variants where applicable. All components must use the established design tokens (colors, spacing on 8px grid, Courier New font family).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Components page contains all reusable TUI elements as proper COMPONENT nodes. Each component has clear naming and consistent styling. Components can be instantiated and used across screens. The design tokens (colors, spacing, typography) are consistent across all components.

sequentialTasks.task: Define design tokens and variable system for the TUI
sequentialTasks.description: COMPLETED. Created `slop-funnel` variable collection (ID 0:319) with 37 COLOR type variables organized into semantic categories: bg-* (16 background tokens), text-* (18 text/status tokens), dot-* (3 window dot tokens), border-* (1 structural token). All 115 colored nodes in Screen1 are bound to these tokens — zero hardcoded colors remain. Tokens exported as CSS custom properties via design_to_tokens. Documented in WIP.md with full token table including IDs and node bindings. Note: spacing and typography tokens are deferred to component library creation (Task 8) since OpenPencil does not support FLOAT/STRING variable binding for layout properties — these will be enforced through component structure rather than variables.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: OpenPencil has named variable collections for colors. All nodes in Screen1 are bound to these variables (not hardcoded values). Changing a variable updates all bound nodes. The token system is documented in WIP.md.

sequentialTasks.task: Define TUI interaction patterns and keyboard navigation model
sequentialTasks.description: Document the keyboard-driven interaction model: (1) Global keybindings (j/k navigation, Enter select, ? help, q quit, Esc back). (2) Screen-specific keys. (3) How focus is indicated visually (highlight, cursor). (4) How the chunk review flow works (select chunk → see diff → merge/discard/note → next). (5) How agent status is displayed during processing. (6) How conflicts are surfaced and resolved. This documentation goes in WIP.md and should inform the visual design (e.g. focus indicators, key hints in StatusBar).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: WIP.md contains a complete interaction model document covering all screens, all keybindings, focus indicators, and workflow transitions. The visual design on all screens reflects these patterns (key hints, focus states, navigation indicators).

sequentialTasks.task: Final design review: export all screens, validate consistency, document design decisions
sequentialTasks.description: Export all screens as PNG at 2x scale. Run describe on each screen and fix any remaining issues. Validate: (1) All colors use design tokens (no hardcoded values). (2) All spacing is on 8px grid. (3) All components use the component library. (4) Typography is consistent. (5) Navigation flows make sense. (6) All product requirements are visually represented. Document all design decisions in WIP.md with rationale.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All screens are exported and visually verified. Describe tool shows zero errors on any screen. WIP.md contains complete design documentation with rationale for all decisions. The design is ready for implementation handoff.

continuousVerification.task: Validate design changes after EVERY modification to Screen1 or any new screen
continuousVerification.description: After each design change in OpenPencil MCP: (1) Re-run describe on the modified node to check for errors. (2) Export the modified screen as PNG to visually verify. (3) Check spacing against 8px grid. (4) Verify color contrast. (5) Update WIP.md with what was changed and why.
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If describe shows any 'error' severity issues, STOP and fix before proceeding. Zero tolerance for contrast failures, overflow, or broken layouts.
continuousVerification.testCoverage: N/A — design phase. Coverage means: every design element has a purpose, every color is tokenized, every component is reusable.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: DESIGN PHASE: All changes are in OpenPencil MCP. Do not create code files. Ask Hana-san before assuming product direction. Update blueprint.json after every task.
HANA-SAN IS THE PRODUCT MANAGER. You are the designer. Ask questions. Do not assume. The blueprint must reflect confirmed decisions only.

slop-funnel must support claude and other agents via ACP (Agent Communication Protocol). Reference: https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/agent-communication-protocol. This is a confirmed requirement that must be reflected in the design.
